body.title: Nanny Ogg: scheduler + world-interaction dispatcher device
body.result: NannyOggDevice: ScheduleEntry + AgentRegistration + default schedule (weekly audit, alignment review, consequence gate, dreaming); 37 tests green
body.description:
Supersedes T-adc-scheduling-primitive. Implement Nanny Ogg as a rack device — the when/then engine. Responsibilities: (1) internal scheduling — cron, periodic tasks (Friday audit, dreaming cadence, alignment reviews), gate condition monitoring, consequence ticket maturation; (2) external scheduling — Google Calendar integration, real-world time commitments; (3) world-interaction dispatch — routes non-coding tickets to specialized agents (calendar agent, social agent, IoT agent, sysadmin agent); (4) agent registry — knows what agents exist and what ticket types they handle; (5) new agent spin-up — can instantiate a new specialized agent when a ticket type has no current handler.

Nanny is the event/trigger engine: 'when condition C is true, execute action A.' Conditions: time (cron), event (ticket closed), threshold (N cycles), date (consequence gate), external trigger.

**Affected files:** devices/ (new devices/nanny/ rack device), supercedes T-adc-scheduling-primitive (close that ticket on sprint start), Igor internal scheduler → stub or remove
**Design rules:** igor-uses-not-contains; scheduling logic is rule-based not inference; world-interaction agents are registered capability nodes, not hardcoded
**Scope boundary:** IN — internal scheduling, gate monitoring, consequence maturation, world dispatch, agent registry; OUT — coding ticket routing (Granny)
**Test plan:** consequence ticket with past gate date gets surfaced; periodic audit fires on schedule; world ticket (add calendar event) routes to calendar agent; Igor alignment review fires via Nanny schedule not Igor internals
